name: Basic Meat Loaf
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Mix all ingredients except catsup together in a large bowl.
3. Transfer the mixture to an ungreased loaf pan, 9 X 5 X 3 inches, or shape into a loaf in an ungreased baking pan.
4. Spoon catsup onto the loaf and place it in the preheated oven.
5. Bake, uncovered, for 1 to 1 1/4 hours or until done.
6. Remove from the oven and let cool slightly before slicing and serving on a heated platter.
NOTE:
For leftover meatloaf, try one of these variations:
- BARCUCED MEAT LOAF: For four 1/2-inch slices, mix half a cup of barbecue sauce with two tablespoons water in a skillet. Place the slices in the skillet, turning to coat all sides with the mixture. Cover and cook over low heat, brushing occasionally until hot, about 10-15 minutes.
- POTATO-TOPPED MEAT LOAF: For four 1/2-inch slices, prepare instant mashed potatoes for four servings as directed on the package. Place the meatloaf slices in a preheated oven set to broil or 550°F. Broil with the tops 3 to 4 inches from the heat for 5 minutes. Spread the potatoes and sprinkle shredded Cheddar cheese over the slices. Return to the oven and broil until melted, about 2 minutes.
- SOUPED-UP MEAT LOAF: For four 1/2-inch slices, mix half to one can of your favorite condensed cream soup (10 3/4 oz) with a quarter to half cup of milk in a skillet. Heat to boiling, stirring frequently. Reduce heat and place the slices in the skillet, turning to coat all sides with the sauce. Cover and simmer until hot, about 10-15 minutes.
